config.ground_truth_policy: Linear interpolation between adjacent referencePoll samples at the candidate target timestamp.
config.product_candidate_target_policy: Actual DWM qpcVBlank target when valid, otherwise poll elapsed timestamp, matching Phase 1.
config.regression_policy: Pointwise candidate error minus reproduced Phase 1 product baseline error at the same product poll/DWM target.
config.non_product_policy: Candidates using dense referencePoll as runtime input or future target information are labeled non_product.

metadata.ProductName: Cursor Mirror トレースツール

candidates.description: Phase 1 product baseline: product poll anchor, last two product polls, gain 0.75, DWM target horizon.
Hold the product poll position until DWM target.
Single lower gain against the product DWM target.
Single moderate gain against the product DWM target.
Single higher gain against the product DWM target.
Gain by DWM horizon: hold under 4 ms, 0.70 for 4-12 ms, 0.80 for >=12 ms.
Gain by DWM horizon: 0.25 under 4 ms, 0.70 for 4-12 ms, 0.55 for >=12 ms.
Use a fixed 4 ms effective prediction horizon, still scored at actual DWM target.
Use a fixed 8 ms effective prediction horizon, still scored at actual DWM target.
Use a fixed 12 ms effective prediction horizon, still scored at actual DWM target.
Use a fixed 16 ms effective prediction horizon, still scored at actual DWM target.
Damp gain after slow product polls: 0.60 at 33-67 ms, 0.35 at >=67 ms, hold at >=100 ms.
Cap effective horizon when product poll cadence slips: 8 ms at 33-67 ms, 4 ms at >=67 ms, hold at >=100 ms.
Fallback to hold when previous product poll interval is at least 67 ms.
Use product poll speed-collapse stop detection; hold for 16 ms after stop entry.
Use product poll speed-collapse stop detection; restore gain over 250 ms.
When latest hook is no older than 16 ms, use its position as a hold anchor.
When latest hook is no older than 16 ms, anchor at hook position and reuse product poll velocity.
Use latest hook anchor with product velocity only when hook/poll disagreement is at least 2 px and hook age is <=16 ms.
Use latest hook as a hold anchor only when hook/poll disagreement is at least 8 px and hook age is <=16 ms.
Use latest two hook positions for anchor and velocity when hook age <=32 ms and hook dt <=100 ms.
Combine best-motivated guards: horizon gain grid, poll interval damping, product stop decay, and high-disagreement hook hold.
Use latest dense referencePoll before product poll as runtime anchor and velocity source.
Use the future referencePoll-interpolated target position directly; this is a zero-error lower bound, not a product candidate.

hypothetical_scheduler_variants.description: Synthetic 4 ms anchors from dense referencePoll with fixed 8 ms target; this is a scheduler/input-cadence bound, not a product replay.
hypothetical_scheduler_variants.note: Not regression-comparable to product poll DWM baseline because anchors and target policy differ.

hypothetical_scheduler_variants.description: Synthetic 4 ms anchors from dense referencePoll with fixed 16 ms target; this is a scheduler/input-cadence bound, not a product replay.
hypothetical_scheduler_variants.note: Not regression-comparable to product poll DWM baseline because anchors and target policy differ.

hypothetical_scheduler_variants.description: Synthetic 8 ms anchors from dense referencePoll with fixed 8 ms target; this is a scheduler/input-cadence bound, not a product replay.
hypothetical_scheduler_variants.note: Not regression-comparable to product poll DWM baseline because anchors and target policy differ.

hypothetical_scheduler_variants.description: Synthetic 8 ms anchors from dense referencePoll with fixed 16 ms target; this is a scheduler/input-cadence bound, not a product replay.
hypothetical_scheduler_variants.note: Not regression-comparable to product poll DWM baseline because anchors and target policy differ.
